segments.speaker: ATTY_DONOVAN
segments.text: Q. Can you list for me, with specificity, each trade secret you contend was misappropriated in this case?\nA. I mean, it's really the platform as a whole. The code, the models, the way we manage customer data -- it's all kind of one integrated system.

segments.speaker: WITNESS
segments.text: Q. You testified earlier that access to the pre-2018 repositories was tightly controlled. Looking at PX-031, this security roadmap, would you agree that, at least as of 2019, credentials were still shared across teams?\nA. Yes, in some cases that is correct.

segments.speaker: ATTY_HALCYON
segments.text: Q. Did you ever receive any source code directly from Halcyon & Pierce's client?\nA. No. We built our system from scratch, although I read their public whitepapers and marketing materials.

segments.speaker: ATTY_DONOVAN
segments.text:
Q. What exactly do you claim as the trade secrets in this lawsuit?
A. It's the way we run the fab. The parameters, the sequences, the recipes. It's hard to slice into pieces.
Q. So there is no single document or file you can point me to that contains the trade secret?
A. Not one file, no.

segments.speaker: WITNESS
segments.text:
Q. PX-010 is a spreadsheet you prepared, correct?
A. Yes.
Q. And you no longer have the earlier versions of this spreadsheet because the logging feature was disabled?
A. That is my understanding.

segments.speaker: WITNESS
segments.text: Q. How did you identify the trade secrets at issue in this case?\nA. We narrowed it to three deployment playbooks listed in PX-018, each tied to specific enterprise customers and protected behind role-based access and customer-specific NDAs.

segments.speaker: ATTY_GRANT
segments.text: Q. The bid-response logs in PX-012, are those the only place your alleged trade secrets live?\nA. No, it's the whole modeling stack. The logs, the feature engineering code, the tuning logic. It's hard to separate.

segments.speaker: WITNESS
segments.text: Q. What do you contend are the trade secrets in this case?\nA. Three routing heuristics described in Section 4 of PX-004, along with the parameter ranges we tuned for our top twenty shippers. Those values are not published and we locked them behind role-based access in our TMS.

segments.speaker: ATTY_DONOVAN
segments.text:
Q. Is PX-045 the dataset you used to evaluate the performance of your recommendation models?
A. It's one of them. We also looked at a lot of dashboards and ad hoc SQL queries.
Q. And the cohort definitions in PX-045, are those used anywhere else in your business?
A. Yes, marketing uses similar segments.

segments.speaker: WITNESS
segments.text: Q. How are you defining the trade secrets in this case?\nA. The feature weights and thresholds in Appendix A to PX-017, plus the scoring logic described in the confidential spec. Those are not in our public docs.

segments.speaker: ATTY_GRANT
segments.text:
Q. Where do your alleged trade secrets live?
A. It's really the whole ad stack. The bidding, pacing, frequency capping -- it's all intertwined.
Q. PX-061 shows gaps in your auction logs. Do you have any record of how the strategies changed over time?
A. Not a complete one, no.

segments.speaker: ATTY_DONOVAN
segments.text:
Q. You rely on PX-014 to support your high-frequency strategy, correct?
A. Yes.
Q. PX-014 is missing several of the trading days you allege were most profitable, isn't it?
A. That's what our ops team told me.

segments.speaker: ATTY_GRANT
segments.text:
Q. The care gap model you built, is there any input feature that is not derivable from Medicare claims alone?
A. Some features are engineered combinations, but they ultimately come from claims fields.
Q. And those engineered features are described in PX-033, correct?
A. Yes.

segments.speaker: ATTY_DONOVAN
segments.text:
Q. PX-028 is labeled as a furnace calibration export. Does it contain the full history of all recipes at issue?
A. No, some older variants were purged when we migrated systems.
Q. So you cannot show the jury exactly how the recipes evolved over time?
A. Not step by step, no.
